headlines: Drift Protocol hit by massive Solana exploit — ~$200–285M stolen
Circle unveils cirBTC, a 1:1 wrapped Bitcoin for institutions
Riot Platforms reports Q1 bitcoin production and sales
Large options expiry today: ~$1.8B in Bitcoin (plus ETH) could move markets
Crypto ETF flows mixed: Bitcoin funds seeing inflows while Ethereum ETFs pull money out (Apr 1–2 data)

articles.title: Drift Protocol hit by massive Solana exploit — ~$200–285M stolen
articles.summary: Solana-based derivatives protocol Drift confirmed an active security incident that onchain trackers show drained at least $200 million and some estimates near $270–285 million. Drift paused deposits and withdrawals while investigators and security firms trace stolen funds and try to contain the attack.
articles.source: The Block / PYMNTS (coverage of onchain figures and protocol comment)

articles.title: Circle unveils cirBTC: a 1:1 wrapped Bitcoin for institutions
articles.summary: Circle announced cirBTC, a wrapped Bitcoin token that will be backed 1:1 by native BTC and verifiable onchain. The product is aimed at OTC desks, market makers and institutional use and will launch first on Ethereum and Circle's Arc network, pending approvals.

articles.title: Riot Platforms reports Q1 bitcoin production and sales
articles.summary: Bitcoin miner Riot Platforms said it mined 1,473 BTC in Q1 2026 and sold 3,778 BTC during the quarter for net proceeds of about $289.5M, while ending March with roughly 15,680 BTC on its balance sheet.

articles.title: Big options expiry today: about $1.8B of BTC options (plus ETH) could boost volatility
articles.summary: Deribit-settled options worth roughly $1.8 billion for Bitcoin expire today, with concurrent Ethereum expiries of around $300 million. Big expiries often bring short-term volatility as dealers hedge and rebalance positions near 'max pain' strike levels.

articles.title: ETF flows mixed (Apr 1–2): Bitcoin ETFs see modest inflows while Ethereum funds face outflows
articles.summary: Recent daily ETF data show spot Bitcoin funds recording small net inflows while several Ethereum ETFs saw outflows, highlighting rotating institutional demand between BTC and ETH and continued capital flows via ETF wrappers.

articles.title: Cambodia passes new cybercrime law targeting scam centres
articles.summary: Cambodia’s parliament approved a law aimed at shutting down organised online scam centres that have been linked to fraud and crypto-related scams. The law adds enforcement tools and follows international pressure to address cross-border fraud networks.

articles.title: Federal Reserve official Michael Barr warns stablecoins pose systemic risks
articles.summary: Fed Governor Michael Barr said stablecoins bring benefits but also risks—reserve quality, liquidity and money‑laundering concerns—and urged careful rule design under the GENIUS Act to protect financial stability and consumers.

articles.title: Metaplanet adds 5,075 BTC in Q1, becoming a top public-company holder
articles.summary: Tokyo-listed firm Metaplanet disclosed it bought 5,075 BTC in the first quarter, bringing its public‑company holdings to about 40,177 BTC — a notable sign that some public companies continue to accumulate Bitcoin even amid recent market weakness.
